items.title: Not to Alarm Anyone, but Flesh-Eating Screwworms Have Entered the US
items.summary: The USDA this week confirmed the first known infection of the carnivorous fly larva, which feast on the flesh of living mammals, after the United States eradicated the nightmare bugs in the 1960s.

items.title: The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked
items.summary: On Uncanny Valley, we dive into the IPO bonanza that the top AI companies are embarking on to the point where some real estate listings are looking for not just regular old cash, but Anthropic stock.

items.title: Phoebe Bridgers Ditched the Internet to Hype Up Her New Music. It’s Working
items.summary: The indie artist has played a string of surprise, small-venue shows with no phones allowed, prompting fans to piece together clues about a potential upcoming album.

items.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
items.summary: The AI giant's co-founder explained why the company may tap the public market for capital and why tokenmaxxing pushback isn't a concern.

items.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
items.summary: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

items.title: Filtr is a new privacy tool that blocks ads in almost every iPhone and Mac app
items.summary: This popular ad blocker app for iPhones, iPads, and Macs can now block ads from loading inside apps, including web browsers, thanks to a new feature in the latest Apple software.

items.title: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
items.summary: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
items.summary: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

items.title: Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
items.summary: The lower chamber of Congress passed a measure that seeks to halt further military action, in a vote seen as largely symbolic.

items.title: Kalshi is building a prediction markets 'Bloomberg Terminal' for high-end traders, source says
items.summary: The prediction market platform is developing the platform to create a singular platform for its highly engaged traders.

items.title: Bitcoin set to slump to new lows for 2026 after recent sell-off, traders forecast
items.summary: The cryptocurrency is likely to break below its lows hit in early February, traders on prediction market Kalshi believe.

items.title: Morgan Stanley will soon open its trillion-dollar wealth management funnel to AI agents
items.summary: Morgan Stanley's move is one of the earliest instances of a major Wall Street bank opening its platforms to external AI tools.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Arizona AG will seek to revive 2020 ‘fake elector’ case
items.summary: The Arizona attorney general’s office said on Thursday that it will seek fresh indictments against a group of so-called “fake electors” after the state’s highest court refused to revive the 2020 election subversion case. “The Arizona Attorney General’s Office will return this case to the grand jury,” a spokesperson for Attorney General Kris Mayes (D)…

items.title: House passes agriculture and FDA appropriations bill
items.summary: The House on Thursday passed its second fiscal year 2027 appropriations bill, marking a key milestone for GOP leaders as they navigate the annual government funding process while juggling a crowded legislative agenda. The lower chamber voted 213-210 to pass the agriculture, rural development, Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and related agencies appropriations bill. Five…

items.title: US announces science and AI partnership with Japan
items.summary: The U.S. has announced a new partnership with Japan on science and artificial intelligence. Energy Department Undersecretary for Science Darío Gil told reporters Thursday that each country would invest $500 million in the joint venture. “This is the defining moment for the next era of science,” he said. “We’re linking our brightest minds and the…

items.title: A study of 8,300 older adults revealed a surprising salt habit
items.summary: A large study of older adults in Brazil found that adding extra salt at the table is still a common habit, especially among men. While too much salt is linked to serious health problems and faster cognitive decline, researchers discovered that women’s salt-shaking habits were tied more closely to lifestyle and diet.

items.title: Goethe never knew this 40-million-year-old ant was hidden in his collection
items.summary: Scientists examining amber from Goethe’s personal collection discovered three hidden fossil insects, including an extinct ant preserved in extraordinary detail. Advanced 3D imaging allowed researchers to see not only the ant’s outer features but also structures inside its body. The findings offer new clues about the species’ biology and suggest it likely built large nests in trees.

items.title: Scientists finally crack an “undruggable” pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
items.summary: For decades, pancreatic cancer has been one of the most lethal cancers, with few effective treatment options. A new drug, daraxonrasib, targets the KRAS mutation that fuels most pancreatic tumors—something many scientists once thought couldn't be done. In a major clinical trial, the treatment nearly doubled survival for patients with advanced disease and reduced the risk of death by 60%.

items.title: Remote workers feel isolated. Back-to-office mandates are not a fix
items.summary: Making social connection part of job design, whether people work remotely, hybrid or in-person, is key to supporting employees‘ well-being.

items.title: Bumblebees can solve problems on their own
items.summary: With no training, bumblebees can work out how to use a ball like a ladder to feed on sugar from an out-of-reach flower.

items.title: Even quiet black holes create winds, new Milky Way observations reveal
items.summary: New observations suggest the supermassive black hole at the Milky Way’s core is blowing gas away from the central behemoth.
